data.title: Nasdaq Tumbles Over 200 Points As Software Stocks Plunge: Investor Sentiment Declines, But Fear Index Remains In 'Greed' Zone
data.description: The latest data from the CNN Money Fear and Greed index indicated a drop in market sentiment, although the index remained in the "Greed" zone on Thursday. U.S. stocks had a downward trend, with the Nasdaq Composite experiencing a decline of over 200 points due to increased oil prices. The ongoing U.S.-Iran conflict contributed to heightened risk aversion, causing oil prices to rise and prompting a shift to defensive assets, impacting the tech sector negatively. President Donald Trump issued orders for the U.S. Navy to target any ships laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, with minesweepers deployed to clear the area. Following their latest quarterly reports, IBM (NYSE: IBM) and ServiceNow Inc. (NYSE: NOW) saw their stocks plummet by around 8% and nearly 18%, respectively. United Rentals Inc. (NYSE: URI) also saw a significant drop in its shares. More information is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: New Hydrogen will publish a special report on April 28, 2026. Stay tuned for important updates.
data.description: A new study has revealed that the global economy shrank by 3.5% in 2020 due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic. This has led to a loss of approximately 255 million full-time jobs worldwide. The study also found that the global GDP fell by $6.5 trillion, causing a significant economic downturn in many countries. Additionally, the pandemic has pushed an estimated 119 to 124 million people into extreme poverty. The study highlights the urgent need for international cooperation and coordinated efforts to mitigate the economic impact of the ongoing crisis.

data.title: Horizon Capital Wins Real Deals Private Equity Awards 2026 CEE Deal of the Year for Datagroup-Volia-Lifecell Transaction
data.description: In Ukraine's capital of KYIV on April 24, 2026, Horizon Capital, a prominent private equity firm operating in Emerging Europe, celebrated its victory at the Real Deals Private Equity Award 2026. The firm, managing assets worth $1.8 billion, was recognized for securing the Central and Eastern European Deal of the Year. This accolade showcases their success in a significant transaction within the region.

data.title: 2025 ezW2Correction Software Delivers Cost-Effective W-2 Corrections with SSA Approval and Built-In PDF Delivery
data.description: Cut penalties, remove printing expenses, and simplify corrections with a cost-effective W-2C solution. Recently launched in Redmond, Wash., on April 24, 2026, this all-in-one option is designed to alleviate the financial burden for businesses. In the current economy, where every cent counts, rectifying W2 mistakes is made more manageable with this new system.

data.title: CMUH and Partners Unveil Breakthrough Targeted Exosome Platform for Non-Invasive Spinal Cord Injury Repair
data.description: A collaboration between China Medical University Hospital, Ever Supreme International Biotechnology Co., Ltd., and SHINE OUT BIO TECHNOLOGY CO., LTD. has resulted in the development of a platform enabling targeted delivery of BDNF mRNA via intravenous means. This innovation has shown promising results in preclinical models, notably enhancing motor coordination. The breakthrough was announced in Taichung on April 24th.

data.title: Cleaning Services Market to Reach $111.5 Billion by 2030 at 6.5% CAGR - Allied Market Research
data.description: The global cleaning services market is experiencing growth due to increased demand for professional hygiene solutions, rapid urbanization, and the incorporation of AI-driven robotic cleaning technologies. A report by Allied Market Research released in Wilmington, Del. on April 24, 2026, highlighted these trends. Key factors contributing to this growth include a rise in demand for advanced cleaning solutions and the integration of artificial intelligence in cleaning services. The market is expected to continue expanding, with market analysts forecasting significant growth in the coming years.

data.title: IQSTEL (IQST) Stock Jumps 31% After Hours: Here's What You Should Know
data.description: IQSTEL Inc. (NASDAQ: IQST) saw a significant 30.86% surge in after-hours trading on Thursday, reaching $2.46 per share. The company provided a strategic update showcasing accelerating revenue growth and a transformation of its platform to target higher margins. For the fiscal year ending on Dec. 31, 2025, IQSTEL reported revenue of $316.9 million, marking an 11% increase from the previous year. Gross profit also saw a boost of 14.3% to $9.46 million. EBITDA stood at $2.7 million, with net income reaching nearly $2 million. Management anticipates achieving consolidated net profitability within the next year. In the fourth quarter of 2025, IQSTEL reported earnings per share of -$0.89, falling short of the estimated -$0.20. Revenue for the same period totaled $84.22 million, missing the projected $104.75 million by 19.60%. CEO Leandro Iglesias noted, "We are entering a phase where we are rapidly growing revenues..."

data.title: Aspo to publish Interim Report for January-March 2026 on Monday, April 27, 2026
data.description: Investor news from Aspo Plc on April 24, 2026, at 9:45 EEST states that Aspo is set to release their Interim Report for January-March 2026 on Monday, April 27, 2026.

data.title: Resolutions of the General Ordinary Shareholders Meeting of INVL Baltic Real Estate
data.description: On April 24, 2026, the General Ordinary Shareholders Meeting of the closed-ended real estate investment firm INVL Baltic Real Estate convened. Key decisions and resolutions were made during this meeting.

data.title: Shareholders of INVL Baltic Real Estate approved dividend allocation for the year 2025
data.description: During the General Shareholders Meeting of the Company held on April 24, 2026, it was resolved to accept the proposed dividend distribution of EUR 0.09 per share for the fiscal year 2025. The total sum allocated for dividends reaches EUR 0.714 million.

data.title: VEON Boosts Accessibility for Investors by Waiving Depositary Service Fees on American Depositary Shares
data.description: VEON Ltd., a global digital operator, recently disclosed that beginning January 1, 2026, BNY Mellon will no longer charge depositary service fees (DSF) to investors holding VEON's American Depositary Shares (ADSs). This change will lead to a decrease in ownership costs by USD 0.05 per ADS annually. The removal of DSF charges aims to make VEON more accessible to current and potential investors, improving affordability and convenience for all parties involved.
